title: pages CRUD tRPC endpoints
description: Implement `pages.listBySection`, `pages.create`, `pages.rename`, `pages.updateSlug`, `pages.reorder`, `pages.delete` tRPC procedures, following the same permission model as sections.
acceptanceCriteria: `pages.listBySection` (protectedProcedure query) accepts `{ sectionId }` and returns pages ordered by `ordem`

`pages.create` (protectedProcedure mutation) accepts `{ sectionId, titulo, slug }`, validates slug format (lowercase, hyphenated, no spaces), and returns a `CONFLICT` error on duplicate slug within the section
`pages.updateSlug` validates uniqueness within the section before applying
`pages.reorder` accepts an ordered list of page ids scoped to one section and updates `ordem` atomically
`pages.delete` cascades to tabs/blocks/revisions per the decision made in TASK-18/19, or blocks with a clear error if cascade was rejected

steps.description: Create pages router with listBySection and create
steps.details: Create `apps/server/src/trpc/routers/pages.ts`. Implement slug validation with a zod regex (`^[a-z0-9]+(-[a-z0-9]+)*$`).
Add both mutations; `updateSlug` must catch the unique constraint violation and rethrow as `TRPCError({ code: 'CONFLICT' })`.

steps.description: Implement reorder scoped to a section
steps.details: Add `reorder: protectedProcedure.input(z.object({ sectionId: z.string(), orderedIds: z.array(z.string()) })).mutation(...)`, updating `ordem` only for pages belonging to `sectionId`.
Add `delete` following the same cascade/block decision as TASK-18 for consistency.

steps.description: Merge into root router and test
steps.details: Add `pages: pagesRouter` to the root router. Test create with duplicate slug in-section fails, in a different section succeeds; test reorder only affects the target section's pages.

technicalNotes: Keep slug validation identical between `create` and `updateSlug` to avoid divergent rules over time â€” extract a shared zod schema.
